D7 Print L2 Understand Atom

Why Add Black?

cyan, magenta and yellow inks stacked at full coverage still come out muddy brown — a fourth black plate gives the rich black and saves the other inks

In theory CMY at 100% should subtract all light and print black. In practice the inks have small reflectances that never combine to zero, so 100/100/100 comes out a dirty brown around L* 40. Adding a fourth black ink (K) gets clean shadows in a single hit, and lets you remove equal portions of CMY for the same look at less ink — grey component replacement and undercolour removal.

Switch K on and watch the centre of the Venn change from brown to deep black, and the ink-load tally drop as black replaces the CMY in neutrals.
